title: Earnings Call Transcript: Twist Bioscience Q1 2026 sees revenue growth, stock surges
text: Twist Bioscience reported strong Q1 2026 financial results, with revenue of $103.7 million exceeding forecasts by 2.96% and achieving 17% year-over-year growth. Despite missing earnings per share expectations at -$0.50 versus -$0.43 forecast, the company's stock surged 7.79% in premarket trading. The biotechnology firm demonstrated solid operational performance with revenue of $103.7 million, up 17% YoY, and gross margin of 52%, up 4 percentage points YoY. DNA Synthesis & Protein Solutions reached $51.1 million, up 27% YoY, with cash position of $197.9 million. Full-year guidance was $435-$440 million revenue (16% growth). CEO Emily Leproust emphasized the company's repeatable model: We're expanding our addressable markets with disciplined product innovation. The company marked its 12th consecutive quarter of revenue growth, driven by expansion into new product categories like protein expression and antibody characterization. A significant growth driver emerged from AI-enabled therapeutics discovery, which generated over $25 million in orders during fiscal 2025. This new market category, virtually nonexistent in 2024, represents a $1.5 billion serviceable opportunity for antibody discovery services. The company increased operating expenses by approximately $10 million quarterly for commercial team expansion and digital infrastructure improvements. Management remains committed to achieving adjusted EBITDA break-even by Q4 fiscal 2026, while maintaining gross margins above 50%.
